paragraph: Washington Township's single-family market is running hot right now. Thirty-five homes sold over six months at a median of $805,000, and 74 percent of them went for over asking. The sale-to-list ratio sits at 1.07, which means sellers are getting their price and then some. Median days on market is 15, so if a home is priced right and in decent shape, you're looking at genuine competition from multiple buyers. By contrast, the condo and townhouse tier is smaller with only 8 sales at a median of $737,500, though the 87 percent above-asking rate tells you the same story: priced homes are moving fast and attracting real interest. If you're looking at Washington Township, expect to move decisively if you find something that works. Sellers know what they have, and in this market, the best properties won't wait long.
